Germany's 5,000 heat deaths: What the numbers reveal
articles.description: More than 5,100 people in Germany died from heat-related causes this year until the end of June, the Robert Koch Institute reports. But how can researchers prove that someone died because of soaring temperatures?

articles.title: Merz and Macron: A Franco-German week of harmony
articles.description: Friedrich Merz and Emmanuel Macron did everything they could this week to demonstrate a close partnership. This is likely because both fear that the next French president could be nationalist Marine Le Pen.

articles.title: Cities are getting smarter about water and other eco wins
articles.description: From flood-resistant infrastructure to new ways of securing drinking water, cities are adapting to the growing climate pressures of a hotter, drier world. Plus, tourists pitching in to help sea turtles.

articles.title: Unwetter : Fast in ganz Deutschland sind am Freitag heftige Gewitter möglich
Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister
articles.description: Andy Burnham has been confirmed as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The former Manchester mayor has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

articles.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
articles.description: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

articles.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
articles.description: The EU Commission has proposed to weaken Europe's emissions trading system, a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?

articles.title: How AI and satellites help fight wildfires
articles.description: Wildfires are raging across Europe, Canada and beyond. To successfully fight these fires, speed is essential. A German startup uses satellite data and AI to spot fires early.

articles.title: Iran's oil supply threat extends beyond Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: Oil and gas producers in the Gulf are seeking alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as traffic again comes under fire. But is Iran also turning its attention to the pipelines meant to bypass the waterway?

articles.title: KI-Konferenz in Shanghai: China fordert mehr Kooperation
articles.description: China ​positioniert sich als Vorreiter der KI-Entwicklung. Mit einer internationalen Koalition will Peking den Einfluss der USA auf diesem Gebiet zurückdrängen.

articles.title: Trump sät Misstrauen an fairen US-Wahlen
articles.description: War die US-Präsidentschaftswahl 2020 manipuliert? Steckt China dahinter? Donald Trump spricht zu seinen Landsleuten und zeichnet vor den bevorstehenden Kongresswahlen ein düsteres Szenario.

articles.title: Hitze: Mieter in Deutschland brauchen besseren Schutz
articles.description: Hitzewellen werden durch die Klimakrise länger, heißer und häufiger. Die Rekordtemperaturen setzen auch Mieterinnen und Mietern immer mehr zu. Bundeskanzler Merz spricht davon, das Baurecht anzupassen.
